Article: Shinji as a loose cannon and adolescent rebellion
[]: Shinji as a loose cannon and adolescent rebellion             Quote Post by mechashiva » Sat Nov 15, 2014 5:14 pm Pardon me if I don't explain my point very well or if its been discussed already but its just something I'd like to touch on because of the larger "How Evangelion is a deconstruction of mecha tropes" article or articles I'm working on. Maybe its my experience with other non-anime forms of media (particularly I mean cop shows or procedurals or certain types of action movies) but the character type of the "loose cannon": somebody who doesn't play by the rules or circumvents them to get the job done at the repeated chewings out of his boss BUT it seems a lot of the times, the police captain or whoever's in a position of authority is doing the yelling can't really do much about telling Officer So and So he's "on thin ice", blah blah blah because even though he caused property damage, he caught the perp and he's a hero getting his own parade with ticker tape or a key to the city, what have you. There's not really anything Police Commissioner Hardass can do. How does this relate to Evangelion? Well, its just my analysis but Shinji is a loose cannon character but the world he exists in does not reward him for his behavior like a cop show would. In macro, these kinds of movies/tv shows are power fantasies, about characters who get shouted at for not following rules, doing things their own way, disrespecting authority but still coming out on top in the end by not listening to said authority. One of the ways Evangelion is set up (with outside of the loops otaku Kensuke and Toji, mostly Kensuke being envious of Shinji's "favored" status) is to make these what would be glamorous, exciting things seem more stifling, scary, stressful and not all that great. Shinji can barely do what he's asked to do without being injured but he never wakes up in the hospital with flowers or get well cards around his bed. He's almost always alone. When he does something right, the people at NERV don't fall all over themselves, calling him a hero. He just did his job and he should be satisfied with that. With his first Angel fight with Sachiel and Shamshel with Kensuke and Toji in his entry plug, it is pretty clear that Shinji has anger issues, in

Article: Shinji as a loose cannon and adolescent rebellion
 hero. He just did his job and he should be satisfied with that. With his first Angel fight with Sachiel and Shamshel with Kensuke and Toji in his entry plug, it is pretty clear that Shinji has anger issues, in ep. 3 especially he disregards a direct order to retreat from Misato and in a flashback in ep. 4 (the one where he runs away) she is chewing him out over it. I mean, I keep dancing around the point and maybe I'm mistaken in my claim but it seems like in any other mecha anime, this conversation would not be happening or it would be coded to make the main character just seem too cool for rules to apply to him or the ends justified the means. Even just earlier than that in ep. 2 Shinji getting punched in the face by Toji because his little sister being hurt in the fight with Sachiel is something that wouldn't happen in a mecha anime made to make the main character/audience surrogate feel good about themselves. What do I think this is all leading to or why is the show set up to not overly praise Shinji to boost his ego or brush aside his faults or make him seem more of a badass hero because he doesn't follow the rules? Because Shinji is teenager meant to learn how to be an adult. Part of growing up means you don't get a tangible reward for doing something well or you get bailed out for making a mistake and when you're growing up as a teenager, its hard to get rid of that mentality of entitlement, like the world owes you something or if you throw a tantrum, eventually you get your way. Its carried to its logical extreme in EoTV and EoE and I think its telling that in ep. 18 that Gendo takes control of the EVA-01 (taking away his hands) to take care of the rogue EVA-03 and then later Shinji takes it out on the NERV HQ's pyramid by stomping on it and Gendo increases the pressure of LCL in Shinji's entry plug because of his "childish tantrum" (plus its telling that Asuka's go-to insult for Shinji in 3.33 is to call him a "brat") At times, EoE feels like locking an angry child in a room to think about what he's done and he promptly trashes his room and breaks everything because life is not fair. I'm getting a bit sidetracked but I just

Article: Shinji as a loose cannon and adolescent rebellion
") At times, EoE feels like locking an angry child in a room to think about what he's done and he promptly trashes his room and breaks everything because life is not fair. I'm getting a bit sidetracked but I just wanted to comment on it specifically because I re-watched ep. 4 and it struck me as "interesting" that the things Misato says about Shinji after the Shamshel fight COULD be justified in another show but it doesn't there.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Nov 16, 2014 3:48 pm The trick here is that Shinji partly acts the way he does, at least initially (panicking and acting on his own) because he was never trained into it. In a sense that's a deconstruction of the young that never got any military training and "fell" inside the super robot cockpit: turns out a complete rookie that never got any training isn't an advantage at all, and more of a liability since (s)he tends to ignore orders. Although you have to notices that Shinji does get better as the series progress, at least until Bardiel appears...

[]:             Quote Post by mechashiva » Sun Nov 16, 2014 6:12 pm True. If you do something enough times (even if you hate it) you're bound to get better at it just from practice. I'm reminded of something Ritsuko and Misato said (I forget which episode) and I'm paraphrasing but it goes something like: Ritsuko: "Amazing! Its like he was born to do this." Misato: "Too bad he hates it." Even though Shinji is a pilot partially because of his father, it doesn't seem to give him an advantage to piloting as far as nepotism goes or he doesn't have any particular skill outside of piloting that makes it easier for him (I'm thinking particularly of Kouji from "Mazinger Z" with his motorcycle riding and Hikaru from "SDF Macross" with his jet piloting) Something else that occurred to me a little while after I posted the thread: I re-watched 3.33 and I realized something about the DSS Choker and that Mari calls Shinji "puppy boy". At first when she calls him "NERV's puppy" in 2.22 had to do with Shinji being NERV's stooge but then

Article: Shinji as a loose cannon and adolescent rebellion
 realized something about the DSS Choker and that Mari calls Shinji "puppy boy". At first when she calls him "NERV's puppy" in 2.22 had to do with Shinji being NERV's stooge but then I realized something: what do you do with a puppy when you begin training him? Put a collar on him. I still think a lot of the stuff in 3.33 is overkill as far as punishing Shinji but in initiating the Third Impact, it was basically insurance to make sure he doesn't repeat it (but you know that it doesn't exactly work as it was intended) I forget which episode but there is one where Shinji wakes up at night in the hospital and there's some TV program playing in another room about a puppy that knows math and I thought it was kind of a random detail but it fits, likening Shinji with a puppy: he does what he's told and when he doesn't, he gets his nose rubbed in it.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Nov 17, 2014 5:37 am mechashiva wrote: True. If you do something enough times (even if you hate it) you're bound to get better at it just from practice. I'm reminded of something Ritsuko and Misato said (I forget which episode) and I'm paraphrasing but it goes something like: Ritsuko: "Amazing! Its like he was born to do this." Misato: "Too bad he hates it." Even though Shinji is a pilot partially because of his father, it doesn't seem to give him an advantage to piloting as far as nepotism goes or he doesn't have any particular skill outside of piloting that makes it easier for him (I'm thinking particularly of Kouji from "Mazinger Z" with his motorcycle riding and Hikaru from "SDF Macross" with his jet piloting)

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Mon Nov 17, 2014 1:15 pm ElMariachi wrote: But that doesn't change the fact that Shinji doesn't have the discipline and self-control that comes with years of training, that Rei possesses as well as Asuka (although in a lesser extend due to her personality)

[]:             Quote Post by mechashiva » Mon Nov 17, 2014 1:39 pm ElMariachi wrote:

Article: Shinji as a loose cannon and adolescent rebellion
 that Rei possesses as well as Asuka (although in a lesser extend due to her personality)

[]:             Quote Post by mechashiva » Mon Nov 17, 2014 1:39 pm ElMariachi wrote: As for Mari, one interpretation of the "Puppy" nickname is because of Shinji's face that seems to be always melancholic, like a beaten puppy. In Ha she calls him "NERV's puppy" both because of his face and for being part of NERV (and their puppet), and in Q it's only "Puppy Boy" probably because he doesn't work for NERV anymore (even thought neo-NERV still managed to manipulate him)

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Mon Nov 17, 2014 1:47 pm There is also interesting thing... You already reduced Shinji into a "dog" that has to be trained. It's already a level of degradation when he is no longer viewed as a human but as animal that needs conditioning. And what does that mean? You don't train animal for animal's sake but for your benefit. What I'm trying to say is... Shinji is not teached how to live, but how to serve.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Nov 17, 2014 3:54 pm ^ The "training" was a metaphor, Stillborn, what mechashiva means is that due to his past, Shinji as a way of thinking and operating that is to instinctively avoid as much contact (be it physical or emotional) and conflict as possible, and thus that it's difficult to pierce his shell. Compiling_Autumn wrote: That's not to say that Shinji is entirely undisciplined--even Asuka was impressed with his cello playing.

[]:             Quote Post by mechashiva » Fri Nov 21, 2014 2:01 pm S'alright. Also, going along with the "Shinji as a puppy" thing, with the help of Dropbox (though I can't seem to make the images appear in the post itself ) [img]https://www.dropbox.com/s/y1b1ny0489xy2bj/Screenshot%202014-11-21%2013.52.20.png?dl=0[/img] [img]https://www.dropbox.com/s/zlq85892gb1zsla

Article: Shinji as a loose cannon and adolescent rebellion
1ny0489xy2bj/Screenshot%202014-11-21%2013.52.20.png?dl=0[/img] [img]https://www.dropbox.com/s/zlq85892gb1zsla/Screenshot%202014-11-21%2013.57.19.png?dl=0[/img] Yay, overlapping dialogue. Also in ep. 10, Asuka refers to Shinji as a "housebroken male" and "obedient". Demoralizing as her comments are, it is basically Shinji in a nutshell: he does what he's told but not because he's "trained" well: he just fears the pain of punishment.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Nov 22, 2014 8:42 am Take in account that Asuka said that only to hurt Shinji because she felt threatened by him, and that ultimately she was even more of an obedient puppy than him, since she fulfilled her role as angel-killing machine to pave the way for the HIP until the end, and when she "turned coats" in EoE was no more than a nuisance for SEELE and their Harpies. Besides Shinji can very well disobey direct orders when something isn't right: see Shamshel or Bardiel.

[]:             Quote Post by mechashiva » Sat Nov 22, 2014 10:58 am I'm not saying he doesn't and Asuka's really not the one to talk about who's "obedient" but she sees herself as kind of the maverick of the EVA pilots and Shinji is too dull and boring to be part on the same level as her so likening him to a whipped puppy is just another way to insult him for not taking initiative and not "being a man"

[]:             Quote Post by LordThaeon » Sat Nov 22, 2014 11:50 pm I disagree. Shinji is rarely disobedient, even when he should take a stand, he passively lets others have their way with him even to where he gets degraded. Just look at his one-sided interactions with Misato and Asuka, hell he didn't even retaliate or object to Rei's undeserved slap, he just stood there and took it. He didn't even hold her accountable later and tell her that what she did was wrong or that his father is not worthy of

Article: Shinji as a loose cannon and adolescent rebellion
, hell he didn't even retaliate or object to Rei's undeserved slap, he just stood there and took it. He didn't even hold her accountable later and tell her that what she did was wrong or that his father is not worthy of her respect. The few times where he actually is disobedient and gets talked down for being "a child", he was actually in the right of things. When he disobeyed Misato during the Shamshel and attacked when told to retreat, that's really the best that he could have done. Eva was low on power and retreating would just expose himself to getting caught and flung around till he ran out of power and was an easier target. Attacking the angel head-on was a risk as shown when Unit 01 was impaled, but given the circumstances, it was a more viable option than turning your back to an opponent with a range advantage over you. But does Misato reanalyze the situation and conclude that Shinji was right? Perhaps come to an agreement that Shinji was right this time, but he still needs to respect the chain of command and obey orders for his own good? Nope. She bashes and lashes out at him and even goes as far as to tell him to leave... When he's the only available pilot at that time. But no! It's Shinji whose wrong for not being a door mat. Then the Bardiel incident was a grand example of Gendo's failure as a commander when he spaces out the evas where Bardiel can pick them off one by one. But no, it's Shinji whose in the wrong for objecting to the maiming/murder of another pilot who was his friend. It's Shinji whose acting like a brat and needs to "grow up". Then Gendo kicks Shinji out even though the kid is the most essential part of his self-righteous suicide plot and was literally seconds away from getting destroyed by Zeruel before Shinji came back. If anything, it's people like Gendo, Misato and Asuka who are acting like brats who need to grow up. But instead of holding them accountable or demanding for them to grow up, the story focuses on and punishes the character whose actually making an effort to better himself. But that's just how I see it. Good find either way.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Nov 23, 2014 6:55 am ^ I think that Gendo being a huge

Article: Shinji as a loose cannon and adolescent rebellion
. But that's just how I see it. Good find either way.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Nov 23, 2014 6:55 am ^ I think that Gendo being a huge hypocrite with the whole Bardiel debacle was completely intentional, to show us how utterly obsessed by his final goal he is, to the point to have shed out most of his humanity and common sense (you just can't ask to a rookie pilot to kill another human being just because an Angel is taking it hostage and expect him to obey, hell even Rei was hesitating to follow though his orders!) For disobeying Misato's order against Shamshel, she told Shinji to fall back toward the mountains so he could recharge the batteries, drop off Touji and Kensuke that were making interferences with the Eva synchronization and think of another attack plan, and they were still out of reach from Shamshel's laser whips, so yes, Misato's plan was the sensible thing to do. That doesn't mean that lashing out at Shinji for disobeying orders like she did was right, simply because she expected him to behave like a trained soldier while he's still at the level of a militiaman at best, she even lampshades it later when Shinji ran off for a couple of days. (Rebuild made it even more blatant by having her stopping herself dead while lecturing Shinji and slapping herself at the realization that she can't have that kind of expectation with Shinji as if he was as trained as Rei or Asuka) As for Shinji's passivity, well yes, that's part of his character, simply because he isolated himself so much from other people that he doesn't know how to react or interact with others.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Sun Nov 23, 2014 6:58 am ^And because of that the few times he takes action on his own he tends to go....overboard (see all of 3.0)

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Nov 23, 2014 7:05 am ^ To be fair 3.0 (and the end of 2.0 for that matter) was more a matter of the universe seemingly having it against him personally! More seriously, it's more because Gendo manipulated everything behind his back so the actions he would take would further his plans,

Article: Shinji as a loose cannon and adolescent rebellion
 the end of 2.0 for that matter) was more a matter of the universe seemingly having it against him personally! More seriously, it's more because Gendo manipulated everything behind his back so the actions he would take would further his plans, which included Avakening EVA-01 in 2.0 and Eva-13 as well as getting rid of Kaworu in 3.0. The real moment of overboard reaction that lend to a catastrophe was when he decided to go for the spears against Kaworu's pleas. His refusal to fight Bardiel could had been one, but the Dummy System prevented it. (even thought Gendo handled the motivation for Shinji in the worst manner ever)

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Sun Nov 23, 2014 7:13 am I'm not denying that there was a lot of manipulation from Gendo there but Shinji's previous constant passivity and isolation played into it a lot.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Nov 23, 2014 11:14 am ^ Of course, and Gendo exploited it perfectly to make him do what he wanted. Hell, in the Rebuild it's even implied that he purposefully crafted Shinji's childhood to mold him into that passive and isolated person!
